definition: proceeding in a gradual, subtle way but with harmful effects
sentence: The self-doubt she described was __________ — it crept in quietly, in the form of small reasonable-sounding thoughts that added up to paralysis.

definition: done, occurring, or active at night
sentence: She had become almost __________ that year — most alive after midnight when the world stopped watching and she could finally think.

definition: not openly acknowledged or displayed; secret
sentence: She preferred to operate __________, gathering information silently while everyone assumed she was too busy glittering to pay attention.

definition: kept secret or done secretively, especially because illicit
sentence: There was something __________ about the feeling — a secret joy kept close, shared with no one, too fragile for daylight.

definition: anxious or fearful that something bad or unpleasant will happen
sentence: She was deeply __________ about the song's release, knowing it would reveal more of herself than anything she'd made before.

definition: having a meaning that is mysterious or obscure; using code or symbols
sentence: She delivered the accusations in a __________ way — clear enough to wound, obscure enough to deny, which was its own kind of cruelty.

definition: engaged in deep thought; appearing darkly thoughtful or menacing
sentence: The song had a __________ quality — circling the same image over and over, too fixated to let the memory go.

definition: the examination of one's own mental and emotional processes
sentence: The album was an exercise in sustained __________, examining every uncomfortable corner of her own personality under harsh fluorescent light.

definition: attempting to avoid notice or attention, typically because of guilt
sentence: She stole __________ glances at the life she hadn't chosen, wondering who she would have been if she'd made different decisions at nineteen.

definition: resembling a labyrinth; intricate and complex
sentence: The process of falling in love again was __________ — full of false turns and dead ends and moments of being utterly lost.

definition: a mental condition characterized by delusions of persecution; unjustified suspicion
sentence: She was no stranger to the particular __________ of fame — the certainty, at certain hours, that everyone was watching and no one understood.

definition: having a harmful effect, especially in a gradual or subtle way
sentence: The silence between them had grown __________ — subtle and slow at first, but corrosive in the way the quietest conflicts always are.

definition: to isolate or hide away; to keep apart from others
sentence: She had found someone who allowed her to __________ herself from the noise — to exist, for a few hours, as a private person.

definition: keeping careful watch for possible danger or difficulties
sentence: She was __________ in the way only someone who'd been burned before can be — watching everything, filing everything, waiting.

definition: subject to sudden or unpredictable changes of mood; volatile and energetic
sentence: She had always been __________, her moods shifting without warning — craving the bright, loud life one moment and aching for stillness the next.

definition: done consciously and intentionally; careful and unhurried
sentence: Everything she did was __________, calculated to appear spontaneous while being precisely engineered — every detail already accounted for.

definition: giving the impression that something bad or unpleasant is about to happen
sentence: There was something quietly __________ in the way she smiled through the whole song — the look of someone who already knows how the story ends.

definition: to cause surprise or confusion in someone; to prove wrong
sentence: She had learned to __________ her critics by being two steps ahead — doing the unexpected thing before they could tell her she couldn't.

definition: not clearly expressed or easily understood; not well known
sentence: She chose to keep the specifics __________, giving just enough detail to be devastating and not enough to be prosecuted.

definition: very weak or slight; not firmly held or maintained
sentence: Her hold on the past was __________ but she gripped it anyway, unwilling to let the regret dissolve before she'd learned what it had to teach.
